summary: Improved lbmthp matching by typing static data, THP decode state, and Movieplayer file/buffer fields, then restructuring several lbmthp helpers around those types and names. The PR also fixed the THPVideoDecode fourth parameter from an integer-style argument to a void pointer and updated the lb call site.

key_files.role: Main matching work for lbmthp helpers, Movieplayer accesses, assert/report calls, frame buffering, decode calls, and rate-table frame computation.
Typed THP decode declarations and Movieplayer/THPDecComp fields such as frame_buffers, curr_file_offset, file_entrynum, rate_table, and THPDec_8032FD40_Data.
Typed lbmthp static data, string labels, Movieplayer storage alias, lbMthp_DataBlock layout, and SObj descriptor data.
Updated the THPVideoDecode call to pass the fourth argument as a void pointer.

what_changed.details: Changed THPDec_8032FD40 to take THPDec_8032FD40_Data* and THPVideoDecode to take void* as its fourth argument; lb_01F8.c now casts lbl_804335B8.unk94 to void* instead of s32.

Renamed several previously unknown fields to frame_buffers, curr_file_offset, file_entrynum, and rate_table, and replaced the split unk_9C/unk_A0/unk_A2/unk_A4 layout with a THPDec_8032FD40_Data field.
Replaced many inline string literals and extern-only data with named static char arrays, a typed lbMthp_DataBlock with STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x250), and a typed lbMthp_803BAFE8 descriptor.
Added LBMTHP_ASSERT and LBMTHP_ASSERTREPORT macros around __assert and OSReport so lbmthp.c can reference named file/message data such as lbl_803BADB0, lbl_803BADBC, lbl_803BADC8, and lbl_803BADEC.

Changed cancelflag from bool to int, introduced a local streamPlayer pointer to Movieplayer, used named fields for current file offset, frame buffers, and file entry number, and reshaped the request_done path.

Used file_entrynum for DVD/lbFile reads, moved warning/report text through static labels, initialized the typed THP decode state, and assigned frame buffer pointers plus Y/UV decode buffers using named fields.
Updated THPVideoDecode source-buffer casts and HSD_DevComRequest arguments to use frame_buffers, curr_file_offset, file_entrynum, and LBMTHP_ASSERTREPORT.
Factored repeated rate-table frame conversion into static inline lbMthp_GetFrame and used the newly named rate_table field.
Gave lbMthp_8001F410 semantic parameter names, used streamPlayer/power locals, stored rate_table, and rewrote lbMthp_8001F67C through a streamPlayer alias plus PAD_STACK for texture setup matching.

smart_moves: Typing the decode-state blob as THPDec_8032FD40_Data let the code express the actual THPDec_8032FD40 call shape instead of juggling adjacent unk fields.
Using a local streamPlayer alias for the Movieplayer global made several functions read naturally while also matching the likely base-register/codegen pattern.
Moving assert and OSReport text into exact static char symbols helped match data and avoided relying on compiler-emitted string literal placement.
The LBMTHP_ASSERTREPORT macro preserved the OSReport-then-__assert behavior with named static file/message arguments, which is more match-friendly than generic HSD_ASSERTREPORT usage here.
Extracting lbMthp_GetFrame removed repeated hand-written rate-table logic while still targeting inline codegen inside fn_8001F2A4.
Correcting THPVideoDecode's fourth argument to void* fixed both the header contract and the lb_01F8.c/lbmthp.c call sites that were treating buffer addresses as integers.
decomp_lessons: When static data is adjacent and address-sensitive, model the block with exact char array sizes and a STATIC_ASSERT before replacing literals with named symbols.
Prefer semantic field names once usage is clear from call sites: HSD_DevComRequest made file_entrynum, curr_file_offset, currPackedSize, and frame_buffers much more evident.
Pointer-vs-integer prototype fixes can have broad matching impact because they remove casts at every caller and change register/type expectations.
For large globals, introducing a local pointer alias can be both clearer C and a matching tactic for PowerPC global access patterns.
Small control-flow artifacts such as a goto label, assignment expressions, unsigned temporaries, and PAD_STACK can matter when chasing the last few unmatched bytes.
Keep unknown names where evidence is weak; this PR only renamed fields with direct supporting usage and left many unk_* members intact.
naming_conventions: Global storage was address-named as lbl_804333E0 while preserving the module-facing Movieplayer name via a macro alias.
Anonymous or address-derived static strings kept lbl_803Bxxxx names, while one obvious warning string used str_Warning_frame_offsets_not_supported.
Movie-player buffer/file fields received role names: frame_buffers, curr_file_offset, currPackedSize, file_entrynum, and rate_table.
Public/helper parameters were clarified by role in lbMthp_8001F410: filename, rate_table, buf, heap_size, and loop.
The typed THP decode substructure uses the existing library-style name THPDec_8032FD40_Data rather than inventing a more speculative semantic name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace repeated Movieplayer global references with struct lbl_804333E0_t* streamPlayer locals.
Use LBMTHP_ASSERT/LBMTHP_ASSERTREPORT to force __assert/OSReport call structure and static message references.
Use assignment expressions such as streamPlayer->unk_138 = (var_r4 = 0) and streamPlayer->unk_110 = (var_r4 = 1) to influence register lifetimes and store order.
Preserve branch shape with a request_done label/goto in fn_8001E910.
Use exact pointer casts for THPVideoDecode source buffers: void* rather than s32.
Keep alignment expressions in forms that match surrounding codegen, including ALIGN_32(...) and explicit (value + 0x1F) & 0xFFFFFFE0 forms.
Use PAD_STACK where needed in lbmthp helpers to maintain stack layout.
Make lbMthp_GetFrame static inline so repeated frame-rate-table conversion can be shared at source level without introducing a new external call.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were captured in this PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code 67.16% (+0.01%, +476 bytes) and matched data 39.41% (+0.05%, +592 bytes).
The bot listed 3 new matches: lbMthp_8001F67C, lbMthp_8001F624, and main/melee/lb/lbmthp .data.
The bot listed improvements to fn_8001E910, fn_8001F2A4, fn_8001ECF4, .sdata, and lbMthp_8001F410.
The PR test plan was python configure.py --wrapper wine && ninja.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching THPVideoDecode or THPDec_8032FD40_Data to see whether the void* and typed decode-state signatures propagated cleanly.
Search lbmthp.c for remaining unk_* fields near frame_buffers, curr_file_offset, file_entrynum, and rate_table that may now be inferable.
Look for other modules where HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTREPORT should be replaced with local __assert/OSReport macros to match static string data.
Investigate the remaining unmatched portions reported by decomp.dev: fn_8001E910 at 99.15%, fn_8001F2A4 at 98.35%, fn_8001ECF4 at 96.88%, and lbMthp_8001F410 at 95.67%.
